records.ety: [L. expatiatus, exspatiatus, p. p. of expatiari, exspatiari, to expatiate; ex out + spatiari to walk about spread out, fr. spatium space. See Space.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Expatiated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Expariating (?).]

defs: To range at large, or without restraint.
quotes: Bids his free soul expatiate in the skies.

defs: To enlarge in discourse or writing; to be copious in argument or discussion; to descant.
quotes: He expatiated on the inconveniences of trade.

defs: To expand; to spread; to extend; to diffuse; to broaden.
quotes: Afford art an ample field in which to expatiate itself.
